history.text: I have burnt my house down. Now I will burn the house of anyone who wants to follow me.
history.author: Kabir Das

history.text: I'm learning that love is a kind of attention that doesn't require a performance
history.author: Saeed Jones

history.text: Your body is keeping a tally of every time you chose to be available instead of rested.
history.author: Original

history.text: Nani said to ignore the poetry and watch if he stays to help wash the heavy kadhais after the guests have gone home.
history.author: Original

history.text: The to-do list stays at the desk. It is not allowed to sleep in your bed with you tonight.
history.author: Original

history.text: You’re so busy trying to be worthy of their struggle, you haven't noticed they’re just waiting for you to come home and
history.author: Original

history.text: That tight voice you use when you’re exactly one sentence away from crying on the phone.
history.author: Original

history.text: I’m very interested in the feeling of being bad at something and keeping going anyway
history.author: Greta Gerwig

history.text: A money plant doesn’t argue with the balcony grill. It just finds the gap and keeps climbing toward the light.
history.author: Original

history.text: I am finally okay with being the one who left, even if your mother still asks about me at every wedding.
history.author: Original

history.text: It’s a specific kind of lonely when the person you want to vent to is the problem.
history.author: Original

history.text: You are trying to solve a Tuesday that hasn’t even happened yet.
history.author: Original

history.text: You don’t owe your productivity a refund for the rest you already took.
history.author: Original

history.text: I think perfection is ugly. Somewhere in the things humans make, I want to see scars, failure, disorder, distortion
history.author: Yohji Yamamoto

history.text: The pressure to 'become someone' stops at the door. Inside, you’re just a person putting the cooker on.
history.author: Original

history.text: You still answer the phone with your successful voice when your mother calls, then sit back down in a room that knows yo
history.author: Original

history.text: The sweat on your face is more honest than the promises you made to yourself at midnight.
history.author: Original

history.text: Your future is built in the boring hours between two and five on a Tuesday afternoon.
history.author: Original

history.text: You can miss someone and still be relieved they're gone. Both things fit in the same mouth.
history.author: Original

history.text: I mistook their chaos for depth. I was young and had no hobbies.
history.author: Hannah Gadsby

history.text: It’s the way I start apologizing the moment someone is actually kind to me
history.author: Original

history.text: Rest is not a betrayal of the people who were never allowed to stop.
history.author: Original

history.text: I don't think you can be a truly good person until you realize how easily you could be a bad one
history.author: Taylor Tomlinson

history.text: Sometimes the best part of a long day is the five minutes in the auto before you have to go inside.
history.author: Original

history.text: The hardest part isn't watching them leave. It's the drawer you still won't empty.
history.author: Original

history.text: She still has your hoodie. You still check her stories. Neither means what it used to.
history.author: Original

history.text: You are still a promising kid in your own head, but the auto driver and the bank manager have already started calling yo
history.author: Original

history.text: One day you'll realise your father started asking your opinion because he ran out of people older than him to ask.
history.author: Florence Welch

history.text: You won’t know you’re becoming stronger until you ignore the notification that used to ruin your dinner.
history.author: Original

history.text: You made a joke about it in the group chat because crying would have required a phone call.
history.author: Original

history.text: It’s not that the stranger was kind; you were just one ‘beta’ away from crying in the rickshaw.
history.author: Original

history.text: You didn’t notice the exact Tuesday your school bag became a laptop sleeve and your afternoon nap became a meeting you c
history.author: Original

history.text: Nobody tells you that discipline is you, a cold cup of chai, and a silent phone.
history.author: Original

history.text: There’s a difference between attention and care. Anyone can text ‘get well soon,’ but only one person brings adrak-chai
history.author: Original

history.text: You won’t see the effort gap until you stop texting first and your phone stays silent all Sunday.
history.author: Original

history.text: One day you’ll realise the noise isn't the traffic; it’s the voice in your head arguing with the car in front.
history.author: Original

history.text: The hardest part isn’t being unemployed. It’s the way your mother lowers the TV volume the moment you walk in with your
history.author: Original

history.text: Inspiration is a word used by people who aren't really doing anything
history.author: Nick Cave
